verdict.summary: Guide is mostly sound for children/grandchildren by descent, but the Sephardic section and 2026 nationality-law status contain critical factual/timing errors and the IRN fee/timeline figures are misleading.

issues.current: 03.05.2026 Президент Сегуру промульгировал Decreto AR 48/XVII ... На 13.05.2026 закон ВСЁ ЕЩЁ НЕ опубликован в DRE
issues.problem: As of the requested review date April 2026, this is future-dated and should not be presented as fact. The correct status as of April 2026 is that the new nationality law was approved by Parliament on 01.04.2026 and was awaiting promulgation/publication; it was not yet in force.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "На апрель 2026 новый закон о гражданстве был одобрен Assembleia da República 01.04.2026, но ещё ожидал промульгации Президентом и публикации в Diário da República. До публикации и вступления в силу применяются действующие правила Lei 37/81 и Regulamento da Nacionalidade."
issues.source: Decreto da Assembleia da República aprovado em 01.04.2026; Lei da Nacionalidade, Lei 37/81

issues.current: До публикации в DRE сефардский способ ещё технически работает в режиме после Decreto-Lei 26/2022.
issues.problem: Misleading/outdated: the current Sephardic route is not merely the post-Decreto-Lei 26/2022 regime. Lei Orgânica n.º 1/2024 materially changed the regime and requires, for new applications, legal residence in Portugal for at least 3 years, consecutive or interpolated, in addition to proof of Sephardic descent/tradition. The guide omits this central requirement.
issues.suggestion: State that since Lei Orgânica n.º 1/2024 the Sephardic naturalisation route requires legal residence in Portugal for at least 3 years, consecutive or interpolated, and that transitional rules may apply only to certain already pending applications.
issues.source: Lei Orgânica n.º 1/2024, de 5 de março; Lei 37/81 art. 6.º

issues.current: Если планируете подать заявление — делайте это СРОЧНО, до публикации Decreto 48/XVII в DRE. Подача закрепляет дело в старом режиме...
issues.problem: This is unsafe legal advice. Filing before a future law is published does not automatically guarantee treatment under a desired 'old regime' unless the enacted transitional provision explicitly says so. Also, as of April 2026 the law was not promulgated/published, and the guide should not rely on alleged presidential remarks as a legal guarantee.
issues.suggestion: Replace with a neutral caveat: "Переходные правила будут понятны только из окончательно опубликованного текста в DRE. Уже поданные дела могут получить переходную защиту только в пределах прямо предусмотренных законом; срочную подачу стоит обсуждать с адвокатом."
issues.source: Lei 37/81; Diário da República publication requirement

issues.current: натурализация после 5 лет законного проживания (по новому закону после публикации Decreto AR 48/XVII в DRE — 7 лет для CPLP/EU, 10 лет для остальных)
issues.problem: This mixes current law with a not-yet-in-force parliamentary decree. As of April 2026, the 7/10-year rule was not in force because the new nationality law was only approved on 01.04.2026 and awaiting promulgation/publication. The sentence could make readers think the change is already settled law.
issues.suggestion: Write: "По действующему на апрель 2026 праву общий срок натурализации остаётся 5 лет законного проживания. Парламент 01.04.2026 одобрил изменения, которые могут увеличить срок до 7 лет для граждан CPLP/EU и до 10 лет для остальных, но они вступят в силу только после промульгации, публикации в DRE и наступления даты вступления в силу."

issues.problem: Likely wrong fee category. Grandchildren of Portuguese citizens under art. 1.º/1/d are attribution of original nationality, not acquisition/naturalisation. The usual IRN emolument for adult attribution applications is €175, while €250 is typical for many acquisition/naturalisation routes.
issues.suggestion: Change to: "для совершеннолетних детей/внуков по atribuição da nacionalidade обычно €175; для некоторых режимов aquisição/naturalização — €250; несовершеннолетние дети португальцев часто бесплатно. Проверять по актуальной Tabela de Emolumentos do IRN."
issues.source: Regulamento Emolumentar dos Registos e Notariado; IRN Tabela de Emolumentos

issues.current: Существенное ужесточение режима действует с 01.09.2022 — через Decreto-Lei n.º 26/2022 ... потребовало доказательств реальной связи с Португалией (проживание, экономические или культурные связи).
issues.problem: Incomplete for 2026. It describes the 2022 tightening but omits the later 2024 organic-law reform, which is now the key current rule for new Sephardic applications.
issues.suggestion: Add after this paragraph: "Позже Lei Orgânica n.º 1/2024 изменила саму Lei da Nacionalidade: для новых заявителей по сефардскому основанию требуется законное проживание в Португалии не менее 3 лет, подряд или с перерывами. Поэтому описание только через DL 26/2022 уже недостаточно."

issues.problem: Maximum of 720 days is optimistic for nationality by grandchildren. IRN nationality files, especially grandchildren and archival/descent cases, commonly exceed 2 years; 24–36+ months is not unusual. A hard 180–720 day range may mislead applicants.
issues.suggestion: Use a wider range or split by category: children with straightforward records may be around several months to 1–2 years; grandchildren often 24–36+ months, sometimes longer depending on IRN backlog, documents and objections.

issues.current: Conservatória dos Registos Centrais (Lisboa) либо консульство.
issues.problem: Too narrow. Nationality applications may be submitted through several IRN civil registry offices/nationality counters, by post to Conservatória dos Registos Centrais in many cases, through consulates abroad, and online in certain cases by lawyers/solicitors.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Подать можно через Conservatória dos Registos Centrais/другие balcões da nacionalidade IRN, консульство за рубежом, а в некоторых случаях онлайн через адвоката или solicitador; конкретный канал зависит от основания и места проживания."

issues.current: Presidência da República — Promulgação Decreto AR 48/XVII ... lastRetrieved: 2026-05-04
issues.problem: The guide is being checked as of April 2026, but this source is dated May 2026 and supports a future-dated claim. It should not appear in an April-2026 verified guide unless the guide explicitly states it was updated after April.
issues.suggestion: Remove this source for an April-2026 version, or change lastVerified/review framing and clearly separate post-April updates from April-2026 legal status.

issues.problem: Metadata conflicts with the requested April 2026 review point and with several sources retrieved in May 2026. This makes the legal snapshot unclear.
issues.suggestion: Either set lastVerified to an April 2026 date and remove future claims, or keep May 2026 but state the guide is updated to May 2026 and re-check all May claims against the DRE and Presidência sources.

missing_topics: Current Sephardic route after Lei Orgânica n.º 1/2024, especially the 3-year legal residence requirement and transitional treatment for pending cases
Clear distinction between current nationality law in force and the 01.04.2026 Parliament-approved reform awaiting promulgation/publication
Updated IRN emolument amounts by exact category: attribution for adult children/grandchildren versus acquisition/naturalisation
Practical caveat that grandchildren files often take longer than 2 years
Submission channels beyond Conservatória dos Registos Centrais and consulates, including IRN nationality counters/post and lawyer/solicitador online submission where available
